cron.untrackedTooltip: This job is in pg_cron but not in the application registry. Add it to lib/cron/registry.ts or to a module manifest.
cron.noMetadata: No metadata. Register this job in lib/cron/registry.ts (core) or in the matching module manifest to add a description.

signInErrors.invalidCredentials: Wrong email or password, please try again.
signInErrors.noAccess: You don't have permission to access the admin panel.
signInErrors.tooManyAttempts: Too many failed attempts. Try again in a few minutes.
signInErrors.banned: Your account has been suspended. Contact support.
signInErrors.deleted: Your account is being deleted. To cancel the request contact support.

general.label: General
general.description: Name, logo, and main information of the application.

email.label: Email
email.description: Customize email templates sent by the system.

snippets.label: Snippets
snippets.description: Manage snippets and textual content of the application.

cron.label: Cron Jobs
cron.description: Inspect, monitor, enable or disable Supabase pg_cron jobs.

languages.label: Languages
languages.description: Manage supported locales, sort order, and native labels. Default locale is set via the I18N_DEFAULT_LOCALE env var.

general.brandAssetsHint: Stored on Supabase Storage. Max 1 MB per file.

logo.label: Logo
logo.hint: Main logo. Shown in transactional emails and the public site header.

favicon.label: Favicon
favicon.hint: Browser tab icon. PNG, SVG or ICO, ideally 32×32 or 64×64.

actionMessages.notificationsRunComplete: Run complete: detected {detected}, inserted {inserted}, emailed {emailed}{dryRun, select, true { (dry-run)} other {}}.
actionMessages.notificationsTestRecipientRequired: Add at least one recipient email before sending a test digest.
actionMessages.notificationsTestDigestSent: Test digest sent to {count, plural, one {# recipient} other {# recipients}}.

mode.registrationsDescription: Users can sign up to the app
mode.maintenanceDescription: All pages are unavailable, a message will be displayed
mode.turnstileText: The sign-up flow is protected by
mode.turnstileNotConfiguredHint: bot protection is silently disabled until you set the keys.

emailTemplates.placeholdersHint: Available placeholders — click to insert into the active field:
emailTemplates.subjectHint: If empty, the default text is used.
emailTemplates.bodyHint: Plain text only — the email HTML is handled automatically. Use the placeholders above.

emailLogo.title: Email logo
emailLogo.descBefore: Choose which brand asset appears in the header of every transactional email. Manage the assets in
emailLogo.optionNone: No image — show app name as text
emailLogo.missingHint: No file uploaded for this slot yet — emails will fall back to the app name until you upload one.

welcome.label: Welcome email
welcome.defaultBody:
Hi '{{userName}}',

Welcome to '{{appName}}'! Your account has been created successfully.

You can sign in to the platform from: '{{appUrl}}'

signup.label: Signup verification
signup.defaultBody:
Hi '{{userName}}',

Use the code below to verify your account.
The code is valid for 15 minutes.

Code: '{{otpCode}}'

reset.label: Password Reset
reset.defaultBody:
Hi '{{userName}}',

You requested to reset your account password.
Click the link below to proceed. The link is valid for 30 minutes.

'{{resetLink}}'

deleted.label: User deleted
deleted.defaultBody:
Hi '{{userName}}',

Your '{{appName}}' account was permanently deleted on '{{deletedDate}}' by an administrator.

Your personal data has been removed from active systems.

waitinglist.label: Waiting list (landing coming-soon)
waitinglist.defaultBody:
Hi,

Thanks for joining the '{{appName}}' waiting list.

You'll be among the first to know when we open the doors — we'll write as soon as we're ready.

See you soon.

emailchange.label: Email change verification
emailchange.defaultSubject: '{{otpCode}}' is your code to confirm the new '{{appName}}' email
emailchange.defaultBody:
Hi '{{userName}}',

You requested to change your '{{appName}}' account email to this address. Enter the code below to confirm the change. If this wasn't you, ignore this email — the change won't be applied.

Code: '{{otpCode}}'

device.label: Device verification (login from new device)
device.defaultSubject: '{{otpCode}}' is your sign-in code from a new device
device.defaultBody:
Hi '{{userName}}',

We detected a sign-in to your '{{appName}}' account from an unrecognised device. Enter the code below to confirm it's you.

Code: '{{otpCode}}'

staffinvite.label: Staff invitation
staffinvite.defaultBody:
Hi,

'{{inviterName}}' invited you to join the '{{appName}}' staff team with the role of '{{roleLabel}}'.

Click the button below to accept or decline the invitation. The link is valid for 48 hours.

gdprexport.label: GDPR export ready
gdprexport.defaultBody:
We've prepared the archive of your personal data on '{{appName}}', as you requested.
You can download it from the button below. The link is valid for 24 hours; if it expires, you can regenerate it from the privacy settings of your account.

accountdeletion.label: Account deletion requested
accountdeletion.defaultBody:
We've received your account deletion request on '{{appName}}'. Your personal data will be permanently deleted on '{{purgeDate}}'.
Until then you can cancel the request by contacting support. After the purge it will no longer be possible to recover the data.

accountdeletionotp.label: Account deletion OTP (OAuth-only)
accountdeletionotp.defaultSubject: '{{otpCode}}' is your code to delete your '{{appName}}' account
accountdeletionotp.defaultBody: You requested to delete your '{{appName}}' account. Enter the code below to confirm. If this wasn't you, ignore this email — the code expires in 15 minutes.

mfaenabled.label: MFA enabled (two-factor authentication)
mfaenabled.defaultBody:
Hi '{{userName}}',

You just enabled two-factor authentication on your '{{appName}}' account. From now on at sign-in we'll ask for a code generated by your authenticator app in addition to your password.

Keep your recovery codes in a safe place: they're the only way to sign in if you lose your phone.

If this wasn't you, sign in to your account, disable two-factor authentication and contact support immediately.

mfadisabled.label: MFA disabled (two-factor authentication)
mfadisabled.defaultBody:
Hi '{{userName}}',

You just disabled two-factor authentication on your '{{appName}}' account. At the next sign-in we'll only use email and password — your account's protection level has decreased.

All the recovery codes you had have been invalidated. You can re-enable two-factor authentication at any time from your security settings.

If this wasn't you, change your password immediately and contact support.

mfaadminreset.label: MFA reset by admin (support reset user TOTP)
mfaadminreset.defaultBody:
Hi '{{userName}}',

A '{{appName}}' administrator reset two-factor authentication on your account. All previous recovery codes have been invalidated and at the next sign-in we'll only use email and password.

Support reason: '{{reason}}'

To restore protection, sign in to your account and re-enable two-factor authentication from your security settings. If you didn't request this operation, contact support immediately and change your password.

notifications.recipientsScheduleSubtitle: Who receives the email digest, how often, and the panel/email severity floor.
notifications.scheduleInstant: Instant — one email per detection cycle
notifications.severityThresholdHint: Alerts below this floor are silently logged for audit only.
notifications.dryRunLabel: Dry-run — log alerts only, never email or notify
notifications.detectionRulesSubtitle: 13 heuristics, all using internal data only — no external geo / VPN services. Tune thresholds per rule.

multiple_ips.label: Multiple IPs
multiple_ips.help: Same user logging in from N+ distinct IP addresses within a time window.

concurrent_devices.label: Concurrent devices
concurrent_devices.help: User has N+ active sessions open at the same time.

burst_creation.label: Burst creation
burst_creation.help: Unusually high number of new sessions opened by the user in a short window.

bot_user_agent.label: Bot User-Agent
bot_user_agent.help: User-Agent string matches a regex of common bots / scrapers / headless browsers.

long_idle_resurrect.label: Long idle resurrect
long_idle_resurrect.help: Old session newly active after being idle for at least N days. Requires Redis.

failed_then_success.label: Failed → success login
failed_then_success.help: N+ failed login attempts followed by a successful login (likely brute-force success).

sensitive_action_new_ip.label: Sensitive action on new IP
sensitive_action_new_ip.help: Account-impacting action (password change, deletion, …) right after a session from a never-seen IP.

new_subnet.label: New subnet
new_subnet.help: Login from a /16 (IPv4) or /64 (IPv6) prefix never seen for that user in the lookback window.

ua_churn.label: User-Agent churn
ua_churn.help: Same user with N+ different User-Agents in a short window — often indicates cookie theft.

cross_user_campaign.label: Cross-user campaign
cross_user_campaign.help: Same IP creating sessions for N+ different users in a short window — credential stuffing pattern.

off_baseline_hours.label: Off-baseline hours
off_baseline_hours.help: Login at an hour outside the user's typical activity window (computed from history).

admin_off_hours.label: Admin off-hours
admin_off_hours.help: Admin user logging in outside the configured business window (UTC hours).

trusted_device_from_fresh_session.label: Trusted device from fresh session
trusted_device_from_fresh_session.help: A trusted device added within minutes of a new session — attacker persisting access.

fields.activityTypeValues: ActivityType values to monitor (one per line)

cron.dbErrorTitle: Cannot read cron jobs from the database.
cron.dbErrorHintBefore: The pg_cron extension must be enabled and the connection user (POSTGRES_URL) must have access to the
cron.coreJobsSubtitle: System-level cron jobs owned by the core (not by any module).
cron.coreJobsEmpty: No core cron jobs found in pg_cron. If you expect some, double-check the jobname matches lib/cron/registry.ts.
cron.untrackedJobsSubtitle: Jobs present in pg_cron that are not registered in any manifest. Add them to lib/cron/registry.ts to give them a description.
cron.untrackedJobsEmpty: No untracked jobs. Every cron in pg_cron is mapped to the core or to a module.
cron.moduleJobsHint: Jobs that belong to an installed module are managed inside the module itself. Open the module page to see and toggle them:

snippets.title: Global snippets
snippets.subtitle: CSS, JavaScript and markup injected on every frontend page.
snippets.emptyDescription: Use "Presets" to add Analytics, GTM or other scripts in seconds.

form.activeOn: Active — will be injected on the frontend

preset.willCreate: {count, plural, one {1 snippet will be created} other {# snippets will be created}}:

ga4.label: Google Analytics 4

gtm.label: Google Tag Manager

hotjar.label: Hotjar

signup.defaultRoleHint: Role automatically assigned to every new user who registers. Roles with administrator privileges are not selectable.
signup.noRolesAvailable: No non-admin roles available. Create at least one role in the Access › Roles section.
signup.consentVersionsHint: Versions are updated automatically every time the page content is modified from the CMS. No manual action is required.

marketing.label: Marketing Policy
marketing.description: Version saved at registration in the accepted_marketing_version field

services.defaultDescription: Configuration of external services (auth, email, anti-bot, cache, CI).

cloudflare.description: Configure Cloudflare Turnstile for bot protection on sign-in and sign-up.

actionMessages.cloudflareTestSecretRequired: Enter the Secret Key before testing.
actionMessages.redisTestCredentialsRequired: Enter Redis URL and token before testing.
actionMessages.googleOAuthTestFieldsRequired: Fill in all three fields before testing.
actionMessages.googleOAuthTestRedirectUriHttps: Redirect URI must start with https:// (or http://localhost for dev).
actionMessages.googleOAuthTestInvalidClientId: Invalid Client ID: must end with .apps.googleusercontent.com
actionMessages.googleOAuthTestInvalidClient: Client ID or Client Secret is not valid.
actionMessages.googleOAuthTestRedirectUriMismatch: Redirect URI not registered in Google Cloud Console for this Client ID.
actionMessages.googleOAuthTestUnauthorizedClient: Client not authorized for the authorization_code grant.
actionMessages.githubCITestRepoTokenRequired: Fill in at least repo and token before testing.
actionMessages.githubCITestInvalidToken: Token is not valid or has expired.
actionMessages.githubCITestBranchNotFound: Branch "{branch}" or file vitest-results.json not found. The first CI run will create it.

resend.apiKeyHint: Stored securely. Never exposed in the frontend.
resend.senderAddressHint: Must be a verified domain on Resend.

cloudflare.siteKeyHint: Cloudflare Dashboard → Turnstile → your site → Site Key
cloudflare.secretKeyHint: Cloudflare Dashboard → Turnstile → your site → Secret Key. Never exposed in the frontend.
cloudflare.infoBoxLine1After: widget is shown on both the sign-in and sign-up forms. If keys are not configured, bot protection is silently disabled.

redis.tokenHint: Find it in Upstash Console → Database → REST API → Read/Write Token

googleOAuth.clientSecretHint: Same page → click on the Client ID → copy the Client Secret
googleOAuth.redirectUriHint: Must match exactly the one registered in Google Cloud Console → Authorized redirect URIs
googleOAuth.infoBoxLine1After: and take precedence over the environment variables
googleOAuth.infoBoxLine2After: with no external dependencies (NextAuth / Auth.js not required).
